title: corchorus incanus subsp. incanus
intro: Corchorus incanus subsp. incanus, a plant species belonging to the Malvaceae family, is a type of jute or mallow plant commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting immune function. In traditional medicine, Corchorus incanus subsp. incanus is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves, roots, and fruits are used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures and decoctions.
